summary: Added a repo-level database of decomp.me Melee scratches at `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` and introduced a Rust workspace tool, `melee-scratch-tracker`, to seed/update that file from the decomp.me API and list scratches by address. The PR formalized a convention where integrated scratches are manually marked `OK` instead of a percent completion. There were no review comments in the slice, so rationale comes mainly from the PR body and diffs.
what_changed: Created `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` with 4362 scratch records in a stable text format: `name = completion:address; // author:... id:... parent:... updated:... created:...`.
Added `tools/scratch-tracker` as a Rust package named `melee-scratch-tracker` in the workspace.
Implemented CLI subcommands `list { addr }`, `refresh`, `seed { from }`, and `update`.
Implemented `cargo run -rp melee-scratch-tracker update` as the documented path for pulling new scratches from decomp.me.
Implemented address-aware listing, demonstrated in the PR with `cargo run -rp melee-scratch-tracker list 0x801450A0` producing name, author, completion, and decomp.me scratch URL columns.
Added parsing and serialization for scratch completion values, including numeric percentages and the special `OK` status for manually confirmed integrated scratches.
Added `Address` states for `TODO`, `INLINE`, numeric `0x????????`, `UNKNOWN`, and `ERROR`, with display formatting preserving uppercase canonical address output.

Added decomp.me API integration against `https://decomp.me/api/scratch` with preset `63`, JSON parsing, pagination, retry/backoff, gzip/brotli support, and update stopping once an already-known creation time is reached.
Added logic to resolve scratch addresses from `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` by symbol name and to heuristically parse 8-digit in-range addresses from scratch names.
Added duplicate-id handling, merge/update logic, parse failure bailouts, and family/parent graph validation and refresh support.
Added many Rust dependencies to `Cargo.lock`, including `reqwest`, `serde`, `serde_json`, `time`, `nom`, `petgraph`, `dashmap`, `rayon`, `backoff`, and logging/CLI crates.
Changed `tools/replace-symbols/Cargo.toml` to depend on `melee-utils` by path only, consistent with the new workspace usage.
smart_moves: Kept scratch data in a plain text config file, making it searchable, diffable, and easy to manually mark as integrated.
Separated tool concerns into focused modules: API fetching, scratch config parsing/writing, address parsing, symbol config lookup, and core scratch model.
Introduced `OK` as a completion value rather than deleting completed scratch records, preserving provenance while distinguishing integrated work from merely 100% decomp.me matches.
Used `symbols.txt` to resolve named scratches to known addresses, making scratch tracking useful even when decomp.me scratch names do not contain explicit addresses.
Sorted saved scratches deterministically by address, completion, timestamps, author, and id via `Scratch::txt_order`, improving reviewability of future updates.
Used update-time/creation-time metadata and duplicate-id checks to make repeated API syncs safer.
Recognized parent/child scratch relationships through `parent:` metadata and added graph checks to catch cyclic family relationships.
Made address listing output compact and column-aligned so decompers can quickly compare multiple scratches for the same function/address.
decomp_lessons: External decomp.me scratch work should be tracked as first-class repo metadata when it can guide matching or integration work.
A scratch database should preserve author, id, parent, created, and updated metadata; this enables attribution, lineage tracking, and safe refresh/update behavior.
Do not treat 100% scratch completion as equivalent to integration into the codebase; this PR used `OK` only after manual confirmation.
Address lookup can combine explicit address parsing with symbol-table resolution, which is useful because scratch names may be human-friendly symbols rather than raw addresses.
For large generated-but-curated metadata files, deterministic serialization and stable sort order are important for manageable diffs.
Tooling that fetches public API data should include retry/backoff, pagination handling, and a bounded update mode rather than blindly refetching everything each time.
Config parsers should fail loudly on malformed records before rewriting, as `scratch_config::load` bails if any scratch lines fail to parse.
naming_conventions: `scratches.txt` entries use `Name = Completion:Address; // key:value key:value ...` formatting.
Integrated scratches are marked with completion `OK`; non-integrated scratches use a percentage such as `100%`, `99.87%`, or `8.33%`.
Address fields use canonical forms including `TODO`, `UNKNOWN`, `INLINE`, `ERROR`, or `0x` plus eight uppercase hex digits.
Scratch metadata keys evidenced in the file/parser include `author`, `id`, `parent`, `updated`, and `created`.
The Rust package is named `melee-scratch-tracker`, matching the invocation `cargo run -rp melee-scratch-tracker ...`.
Tool modules use descriptive Rust snake_case filenames: `decompme_api.rs`, `scratch_config.rs`, `dtk_config.rs`, `address.rs`, `parse.rs`, and `scratch.rs`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `melee-scratch-tracker list 0xADDRESS` to find decomp.me scratches targeting a specific function address before starting or reviewing matching work.
Use `scratches.txt` to identify high-completion or already integrated (`OK`) scratch candidates for a symbol.
Use parent scratch metadata to trace derivative scratch attempts and avoid losing lineage when evaluating which scratch to integrate.
Use symbol-name-to-address resolution from `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to connect decomp.me scratch names to repo addresses.
Evidence for direct assembly matching tactics is weak in this PR; the changes are primarily tracking/tooling rather than function decompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged quickly on the same day it was opened, but the slice provides no reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` to mark scratches `OK` after integration.
Find later PRs extending `melee-scratch-tracker` with TU-wide listing or other planned features.
Search for usages of `cargo run -rp melee-scratch-tracker list` in developer workflow notes or comments.
Find PRs that use scratch IDs from `scratches.txt` as evidence for matching functions.
Review later changes to `scratch_config.rs` for format or parser convention changes.
